name: fsspec (Filesystem Interfaces for Python)

auth.notes: Cloud provider credentials (AWS, GCS, Azure) passed via storage_options

performance.notes: Cloud storage latency; local filesystem ~0ms; caching available

agent_readiness.notes: Unified filesystem interface. fs = fsspec.filesystem('s3'). open(), ls(), glob(), info(), put(), get(). Works with: local, S3 (s3fs), GCS (gcsfs), Azure (adlfs), SFTP, FTP, HTTP, memory, Arrow, HDFS. Used by: Pandas, Polars, PyArrow, Dask, DuckDB (pd.read_csv('s3://bucket/file.csv') internally uses fsspec). open_local() for file-like access to any URI. filecaches for caching remote files locally. Universal file reading/writing across storage backends.

gotchas: Cloud implementations are separate packages (s3fs, gcsfs, adlfs)
Large directory listings can be slow
best_when: Data pipelines reading from multiple cloud storage backends
Making Pandas/Polars read from S3/GCS/Azure uniformly
Libraries wanting filesystem abstraction without cloud-specific code
caching remote files locally for repeated access
avoid_when: Single-cloud apps (native SDK may have better features)
Streaming very large files (native cloud SDKs may be faster)
Simple local file operations (built-in pathlib suffices)
summary: fsspec provides a unified filesystem interface across local, S3, GCS, Azure, SFTP, HTTP, and more. fsspec.filesystem('s3').open()/ls()/glob(). Used internally by Pandas, Polars, PyArrow, DuckDB for reading cloud URIs. Separate packages for each cloud (s3fs, gcsfs, adlfs). File caching for repeated remote access. Essential for cloud-agnostic data pipelines.
